preamble: The voiced aspirated bilabial plosive /bʱ/ can undergo a historical phonetic transformation to the voiceless labiodental fricative /f/. This change is notably observed in the development from Proto-Indo-European to Latin, where the initial Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (*bʰ) became /f/.
phoneticEffects: Deaspiration, Devoicing, Fricativization (Lenition), Place Assimilation (Bilabial to Labiodental)

examples.note: The Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirated bilabial plosive *bʰ regularly became /f/ in word-initial position in Latin.

sources: The First Sound-shifting, Verner's Law, and other Consonant Changes which took place in the Prim. Germanic Language
